### Post by ultranoize on 2007-04-03
I've just installed 6.10 on a virtual machine using Virtual Box. The install went really smooth but I can't get a higher resolution than 1024x768. I know I can go up to 1280x1024 when running Windows XP.  I have done this so far:

```

sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm start

```

This is my xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.

# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg

	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice	"Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice     "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
```

Any suggestions ?? 
Thanks

---

### Post by compmodder26 on 2007-04-03
You are using the "vesa" display driver.  As far as I know, this driver doesn't support high resolutions.  You will need to install the Virtualbox Guest Additions.  This should give you a better display driver that should allow you to use the higher resolutions.  You should be able to follow the instructions in the Virtualbox manual: [http://www.virtualbox.org/download/UserManual.pdf](http://www.virtualbox.org/download/UserManual.pdf).  The section you want to look at is chapter 4.

---

### Post by ultranoize on 2007-04-03
Thanks a lot, it did solve the problem. I just had to install the Guest Additions. 
This is what happens when one does not read the Manual. :lolflag:

---

### Post by zabyss on 2007-05-17
I am having this same problem.  I installed Ubuntu Studio 7.04 through Virtualbox on an XP laptop.  I've read what the Virtualbox Manual has to say about the issue and I've gotten as far as loading the .iso in Ubuntu - it shows on the desktop there.  Going through Devices/Install Guest Additions does nothing and I have no idea what to do from here.  The instructions don't help because I know nothing about Linux yet.  I've also been searching and searching for an easier explanation, but nothing applies exactly.  Can anyone help?

### Post by tshearman on 2008-03-01
I'm having this exact same problem...
     "Device > Install Guest Additions" does nothing... help 
     thanks

I think i figured it out.  Turns out that manual is worth something.  I went in and found the Guest Additions ISO, and mounted it to the Virtual Machine...  we'll see if it works
